column3: # of unique IP addresses viewing job postings on external career site
column6:
Helps calculate Conversion Funnel: View-to-Submision
- We will not be able to differentiate between via logged in account and anonymous
- Use view job posting end point
- We wont be able to tie these users to a specific job posting, but we can do something like this: We say 10 unique users viewing at least one job posting (this metric) > and we saw 8 unique candidates starting an application > and we saw 5 unique candidates submitting an application

examples of how this will work:
- 1. Chelsea views JR1 3 times > we count this as 1 person reached because we care about converting this person to start an application and then submit, regardless of how many times they have viewed that JR 
Erin views JR1 3 times, and JR2 1 time > we count this as 1 person

Rolling 12 months - ie. if we are in June 2024, IUM should look back 12 months.
Dev can support on what "new hire" means - 
Job Offer Accepted (if position mgmt, first position offer accepted) OR 
Hire events completed 
OR hire date/effective date
The above should basically use what is used in the existing IUM as the denominator - devs to document that. This was in relation to Offers alone so sohuld be separate from others going forward, such as the new hire retention below. That should really be what we determine as the true number of hires - and then that should be used as our definition of new hire going forward for new IUMs.
